title: chromolaena moritziana
intro: Chromolaena moritziana, also known as Mexican mint or tree marigold, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to soothe digestive problems and skin irritations. Chromolaena moritziana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies and topical applications to harness its health benefits.
